NKJV.1: After two days it was the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread. And the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take Him by trickery and put Him to death.
NKJV.2: But they said, “Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar of the people.”
NKJV.3: And being in Bethany at the house of Simon the leper, as He sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster flask of very costly oil of spikenard. Then she broke the flask and poured it on His head.
NKJV.4: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, and said, “Why was this fragrant oil wasted?
NKJV.5: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor.” And they criticized her sharply.
NKJV.6: But Jesus said, “Let her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for Me.
NKJV.7: For you have the poor with you always, and whenever you wish you may do them good;  but Me you do not have always.
NKJV.8: She has done what she could. She has come beforehand to anoint My body for burial.
NKJV.9: Assuredly, I say to you, wherever this gospel is  preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be told as a memorial to her.”
NKJV.10: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Him to them.
NKJV.11: And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. So he sought how he might conveniently betray Him.
NKJV.12: Now on the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they killed the Passover lamb, His disciples said to Him, “Where do You want us to go and prepare, that You may eat the Passover?”
NKJV.13: And He sent out two of His disciples and said to them, “Go into the city, and a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him.
NKJV.14: Wherever he goes in, say to the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is the guest room in which I may eat the Passover with My disciples?” ’
NKJV.15: Then he will show you a large upper room, furnished and prepared; there make ready for us.”
NKJV.16: So His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found it just as He had said to them; and they prepared the Passover.
NKJV.17: In the evening He came with the twelve.
NKJV.18: Now as they sat and ate, Jesus said, “Assuredly, I say to you,  one of you who eats with Me will betray Me.”
NKJV.19: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say to Him one by one, “Is it I?” And another said, “Is it I?”
NKJV.20: He answered and said to them, “It is one of the twelve, who dips with Me in the dish.
NKJV.21: The Son of Man indeed goes just as it is written of Him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been good for that man if he had never been born.”
NKJV.22: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them and said, “Take, eat; this is My body.”
NKJV.23: Then He took the cup, and when He had given thanks He gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
NKJV.24: And He said to them, “This is My blood of the new covenant, which is shed for many.
NKJV.25: Assuredly, I say to you, I will no longer drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.26: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
NKJV.27: Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of Me this night, for it is written:  ‘I will strike the Shepherd, And the sheep will be scattered.’
NKJV.28: “But  after I have been raised, I will go before you to Galilee.”
NKJV.29: Peter said to Him, “Even if all are made to stumble, yet I will not be.”
NKJV.30: Jesus said to him, “Assuredly, I say to you that today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times.”
NKJV.31: But he spoke more vehemently, “If I have to die with You, I will not deny You!” And they all said likewise.
NKJV.32: Then they came to a place which was named Gethsemane; and He said to His disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
NKJV.33: And He took Peter, James, and John with Him, and He began to be troubled and deeply distressed.
NKJV.34: Then He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here and watch.”
NKJV.35: He went a little farther, and fell on the ground, and prayed that if it were possible, the hour might pass from Him.
NKJV.36: And He said, “Abba, Father, all things are possible for You. Take this cup away from Me; nevertheless, not what I will, but what You will.”
NKJV.37: Then He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Could you not watch one hour?
NKJV.38: Watch and pray, lest you enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
NKJV.39: Again He went away and prayed, and spoke the same words.
NKJV.40: And when He returned, He found them asleep again, for their eyes were heavy; and they did not know what to answer Him.
NKJV.41: Then He came the third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and resting? It is enough!  The hour has come; behold, the Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of sinners.
NKJV.42: Rise, let us be going. See, My betrayer is at hand.”

NKJV.45: As soon as he had come, immediately he went up to Him and said to Him, “Rabbi, Rabbi!” and kissed Him.
NKJV.46: Then they laid their hands on Him and took Him.
NKJV.47: And one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
NKJV.48: Then Jesus answered and said to them, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to take Me?
NKJV.49: I was daily with you in the temple  teaching, and you did not seize Me. But  the Scriptures must be fulfilled.”
NKJV.50: Then they all forsook Him and fled.
NKJV.51: Now a certain young man followed Him, having a linen cloth thrown around his naked body. And the young men laid hold of him,
NKJV.52: and he left the linen cloth and fled from them naked.
NKJV.53: And they led Jesus away to the high priest; and with him were assembled all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes.

NKJV.57: Then some rose up and bore false witness against Him, saying,

NKJV.60: And the high priest stood up in the midst and asked Jesus, saying, “Do You answer nothing? What is it these men testify against You?”

ESV.1: It was now two days before the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread. And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how to arrest him by stealth and kill him,
ESV.2: for they said, “Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar from the people.”
ESV.3: And while he was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper,  as he was reclining at table, a woman came with an alabaster flask of ointment of pure nard, very costly, and she broke the flask and poured it over his head.
ESV.4: There were some who said to themselves indignantly, “Why was the ointment wasted like that?
ESV.5: For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii  and given to the poor.” And they scolded her.
ESV.6: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a beautiful thing to me.
ESV.7: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want, you can do good for them. But you will not always have me.
ESV.8: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body beforehand for burial.
ESV.9: And truly, I say to you, wherever the gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in memory of her.”
ESV.10: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went to the chief priests in order to betray him to them.
ESV.11: And when they heard it, they were glad and promised to give him money. And he sought an opportunity to betray him.
ESV.12: And on the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrificed the Passover lamb, his disciples said to him, “Where will you have us go and prepare for you to eat the Passover?”
ESV.13: And he sent two of his disciples and said to them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him,
ESV.14: and wherever he enters, say to the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?’
ESV.15: And he will show you a large upper room furnished and ready; there prepare for us.”
ESV.16: And the disciples set out and went to the city and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
ESV.17: And when it was evening, he came with the twelve.
ESV.18: And as they were reclining at table and eating, Jesus said, “Truly, I say to you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me.”
ESV.19: They began to be sorrowful and to say to him one after another, “Is it I?”
ESV.20: He said to them, “It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping bread into the dish with me.
ESV.21: For the Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that man if he had not been born.”
ESV.22: And as they were eating, he took bread, and after blessing it broke it and gave it to them, and said, “Take; this is my body.”
ESV.23: And he took a cup, and when he had given thanks he gave it to them, and they all drank of it.
ESV.24: And he said to them,  “This is my blood of the  covenant, which is poured out for many.
ESV.25: Truly, I say to you, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
ESV.26: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
ESV.27: And Jesus said to them, “You will all fall away, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
ESV.28: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.”
ESV.29: Peter said to him, “Even though they all fall away, I will not.”
ESV.30: And Jesus said to him, “Truly, I tell you, this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”
ESV.31: But he said emphatically, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” And they all said the same.
ESV.32: And they went to a place called Gethsemane. And he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
ESV.33: And he took with him Peter and James and John, and began to be greatly distressed and troubled.
ESV.34: And he said to them,  “My soul is very sorrowful, even to death. Remain here and watch.”
ESV.35: And going a little farther, he fell on the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
ESV.36: And he said,  “Abba, Father, all things are possible for you. Remove this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will.”
ESV.37: And he came and found them sleeping, and he said to Peter, “Simon, are you asleep? Could you not watch one hour?
ESV.38: Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
ESV.39: And again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.
ESV.40: And again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they did not know what to answer him.
ESV.41: And he came the third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? It is enough; the hour has come. The Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
ESV.42: Rise, let us be going; see, my betrayer is at hand.”
ESV.43: And immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas came, one of the twelve, and with him a crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
ESV.44: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “The one I will kiss is the man. Seize him and lead him away under guard.”
ESV.45: And when he came, he went up to him at once and said, “Rabbi!” And he kissed him.
ESV.46: And they laid hands on him and seized him.
ESV.47: But one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the servant  of the high priest and cut off his ear.
ESV.48: And Jesus said to them, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to capture me?
ESV.49: Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me. But let the Scriptures be fulfilled.”

ESV.51: And a young man followed him, with nothing but a linen cloth about his body. And they seized him,
ESV.52: but he left the linen cloth and ran away naked.
ESV.53: And they led Jesus to the high priest. And all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes came together.
ESV.54: And Peter had followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. And he was sitting with the guards and warming himself at the fire.
ESV.55: Now the chief priests and the whole council  were seeking testimony against Jesus to put him to death, but they found none.
ESV.56: For many bore false witness against him, but their testimony did not agree.
ESV.57: And some stood up and bore false witness against him, saying,
ESV.58: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another, not made with hands.’”
ESV.59: Yet even about this their testimony did not agree.
ESV.60: And the high priest stood up in the midst and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer to make? What is it that these men testify against you?”
ESV.61: But he remained silent and made no answer. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”
ESV.62: And Jesus said, “I am, and you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of heaven.”
ESV.63: And the high priest tore his garments and said, “What further witnesses do we need?
ESV.64: You have heard his blasphemy. What is your decision?” And they all condemned him as deserving death.
ESV.65: And some began to spit on him and to cover his face and to strike him, saying to him, “Prophesy!” And the guards received him with blows.
ESV.66: And as Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant girls of the high priest came,
ESV.67: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, “You also were with the Nazarene, Jesus.”
ESV.68: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know nor understand what you mean.” And he went out into the gateway  and the rooster crowed.
ESV.69: And the servant girl saw him and began again to say to the bystanders, “This man is one of them.”
ESV.70: But again he denied it. And after a little while the bystanders again said to Peter, “Certainly you are one of them, for you are a Galilean.”
ESV.71: But he began to invoke a curse on himself and to swear, “I do not know this man of whom you speak.”
ESV.72: And immediately the rooster crowed a second time. And Peter remembered how Jesus had said to him,  “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” And he broke down and wept.

NASB.1: Now the Passover and Unleavened Bread were two days away; and the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how to seize Him by stealth and kill [Him];
NASB.2: for they were saying, 'Not during the festival, otherwise there might be a riot of the people.'
NASB.3: While He was in Bethany at the home of Simon the leper, and reclining [at the table], there came a woman with an alabaster vial of very costly perfume of pure nard; [and] she broke the vial and poured it over His head.
NASB.4: But some were indignantly [remarking] to one another, 'Why has this perfume been wasted?
NASB.5: 'For this perfume might have been sold for over three hundred denarii, and [the money] given to the poor.' And they were scolding her.
NASB.6: But Jesus said, 'Let her alone; why do you bother her? She has done a good deed to Me.
NASB.7: 'For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you wish you can do good to them; but you do not always have Me.
NASB.8: 'She has done what she could; she has anointed My body beforehand for the burial.
NASB.9: 'Truly I say to you, wherever the gospel is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be spoken of in memory of her.'
NASB.10: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went off to the chief priests in order to betray Him to them.
NASB.11: They were glad when they heard [this], and promised to give him money. And he [began] seeking how to betray Him at an opportune time.
NASB.12: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover [lamb] was being sacrificed, His disciples said to Him, 'Where do You want us to go and prepare for You to eat the Passover?'
NASB.13: And He sent two of His disciples and said to them, 'Go into the city, and a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him;
NASB.14: and wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, 'The Teacher says, 'Where is My guest room in which I may eat the Passover with My disciples?''
NASB.15: 'And he himself will show you a large upper room furnished [and] ready; prepare for us there.'
NASB.16: The disciples went out and came to the city, and found [it] just as He had told them; and they prepared the Passover.
NASB.17: When it was evening He came with the twelve.
NASB.18: As they were reclining [at the table] and eating, Jesus said, 'Truly I say to you that one of you will betray Me-- one who is eating with Me.'
NASB.19: They began to be grieved and to say to Him one by one, 'Surely not I?'
NASB.20: And He said to them, '[It is] one of the twelve, one who dips with Me in the bowl.
NASB.21: 'For the Son of Man [is to] go just as it is written of Him; but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! [It would have been] good for that man if he had not been born.'
NASB.22: While they were eating, He took [some] bread, and after a blessing He broke [it], and gave [it] to them, and said, 'Take [it]; this is My body.'
NASB.23: And when He had taken a cup [and] given thanks, He gave [it] to them, and they all drank from it.
NASB.24: And He said to them, 'This is My blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
NASB.25: 'Truly I say to you, I will never again drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.'
NASB.26: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
NASB.27: And Jesus said to them, 'You will all fall away, because it is written, 'I WILL STRIKE DOWN THE SHEPHERD, AND THE SHEEP SHALL BE SCATTERED.'
NASB.28: 'But after I have been raised, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.'
NASB.29: But Peter said to Him, '[Even] though all may fall away, yet I will not.'
NASB.30: And Jesus said to him, 'Truly I say to you, that this very night, before a rooster crows twice, you yourself will deny Me three times.'
NASB.31: But [Peter] kept saying insistently, '[Even] if I have to die with You, I will not deny You!' And they all were saying the same thing also.
NASB.32: They came to a place named Gethsemane; and He said to His disciples, 'Sit here until I have prayed.'
NASB.33: And He took with Him Peter and James and John, and began to be very distressed and troubled.
NASB.34: And He said to them, 'My soul is deeply grieved to the point of death; remain here and keep watch.'
NASB.35: And He went a little beyond [them], and fell to the ground and [began] to pray that if it were possible, the hour might pass Him by.
NASB.36: And He was saying, 'Abba! Father! All things are possible for You; remove this cup from Me; yet not what I will, but what You will.'
NASB.37: And He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, 'Simon, are you asleep? Could you not keep watch for one hour?
NASB.38: 'Keep watching and praying that you may not come into temptation; the spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.'
NASB.39: Again He went away and prayed, saying the same words.
NASB.40: And again He came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy; and they did not know what to answer Him.
NASB.41: And He came the third time, and said to them, 'Are you still sleeping and resting? It is enough; the hour has come; behold, the Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of sinners.
NASB.42: 'Get up, let us be going; behold, the one who betrays Me is at hand!'
NASB.43: Immediately while He was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came up accompanied by a crowd with swords and clubs, [who were] from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
NASB.44: Now he who was betraying Him had given them a signal, saying, 'Whomever I kiss, He is the one; seize Him and lead Him away under guard.'
NASB.45: After coming, Judas immediately went to Him, saying, 'Rabbi!' and kissed Him.
NASB.46: They laid hands on Him and seized Him.
NASB.47: But one of those who stood by drew his sword, and struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his ear.
NASB.48: And Jesus said to them, 'Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest Me, as [you would] against a robber?
NASB.49: 'Every day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize Me; but [this has] [taken place] to fulfill the Scriptures.'
NASB.50: And they all left Him and fled.
NASB.51: A young man was following Him, wearing [nothing but] a linen sheet over [his] naked [body]; and they seized him.
NASB.52: But he pulled free of the linen sheet and escaped naked.
NASB.53: They led Jesus away to the high priest; and all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes gathered together.
NASB.54: Peter had followed Him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest; and he was sitting with the officers and warming himself at the fire.
NASB.55: Now the chief priests and the whole Council kept trying to obtain testimony against Jesus to put Him to death, and they were not finding any.
NASB.56: For many were giving false testimony against Him, but their testimony was not consistent.
NASB.57: Some stood up and [began] to give false testimony against Him, saying,
NASB.58: 'We heard Him say, 'I will destroy this temple made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.''
NASB.59: Not even in this respect was their testimony consistent.
NASB.60: The high priest stood up [and came] forward and questioned Jesus, saying, 'Do You not answer? What is it that these men are testifying against You?'
NASB.61: But He kept silent and did not answer. Again the high priest was questioning Him, and saying to Him, 'Are You the Christ, the Son of the Blessed [One]?'
NASB.62: And Jesus said, 'I am; and you shall see THE SON OF MAN SITTING AT THE RIGHT HAND OF POWER, and COMING WITH THE CLOUDS OF HEAVEN.'
NASB.63: Tearing his clothes, the high priest said, 'What further need do we have of witnesses?
NASB.64: 'You have heard the blasphemy; how does it seem to you?' And they all condemned Him to be deserving of death.
NASB.65: Some began to spit at Him, and to blindfold Him, and to beat Him with their fists, and to say to Him, 'Prophesy!' And the officers received Him with slaps [in the face].
NASB.66: As Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant-girls of the high priest came,
NASB.67: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, 'You also were with Jesus the Nazarene.'
NASB.68: But he denied [it], saying, 'I neither know nor understand what you are talking about.' And he went out onto the porch, and a rooster crowed.
NASB.69: The servant-girl saw him, and began once more to say to the bystanders, 'This is [one] of them!'
NASB.70: But again he denied it. And after a little while the bystanders were again saying to Peter, 'Surely you are [one] of them, for you are a Galilean too.'
NASB.71: But he began to curse and swear, 'I do not know this man you are talking about!'
NASB.72: Immediately a rooster crowed a second time. And Peter remembered how Jesus had made the remark to him, 'Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times.' And he began to weep.

NLT.1: It was now two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The leading priests and the teachers of religious law were still looking for an opportunity to capture Jesus secretly and kill him.
NLT.2: “But not during the Passover celebration,” they agreed, “or the people may riot.”
NLT.3: Meanwhile, Jesus was in Bethany at the home of Simon, a man who had previously had leprosy. While he was eating, a woman came in with a beautiful alabaster jar of expensive perfume made from essence of nard. She broke open the jar and poured the perfume over his head.
NLT.4: Some of those at the table were indignant. “Why waste such expensive perfume?” they asked.
NLT.5: “It could have been sold for a year’s wages and the money given to the poor!” So they scolded her harshly.
NLT.6: But Jesus replied, “Leave her alone. Why criticize her for doing such a good thing to me?
NLT.7: You will always have the poor among you, and you can help them whenever you want to. But you will not always have me.
NLT.8: She has done what she could and has anointed my body for burial ahead of time.
NLT.9: I tell you the truth, wherever the Good News is preached throughout the world, this woman’s deed will be remembered and discussed.”
NLT.10: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve disciples, went to the leading priests to arrange to betray Jesus to them.
NLT.11: They were delighted when they heard why he had come, and they promised to give him money. So he began looking for an opportunity to betray Jesus.
NLT.12: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb is sacrificed, Jesus’ disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go to prepare the Passover meal for you?”
NLT.13: So Jesus sent two of them into Jerusalem with these instructions: “As you go into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him.
NLT.14: At the house he enters, say to the owner, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples?’
NLT.15: He will take you upstairs to a large room that is already set up. That is where you should prepare our meal.”
NLT.16: So the two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had said, and they prepared the Passover meal there.
NLT.17: In the evening Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
NLT.18: As they were at the table eating, Jesus said, “I tell you the truth, one of you eating with me here will betray me.”
NLT.19: Greatly distressed, each one asked in turn, “Am I the one?”
NLT.20: He replied, “It is one of you twelve who is eating from this bowl with me.
NLT.21: For the Son of Man must die, as the Scriptures declared long ago. But how terrible it will be for the one who betrays him. It would be far better for that man if he had never been born!”
NLT.22: As they were eating, Jesus took some bread and blessed it. Then he broke it in pieces and gave it to the disciples, saying, “Take it, for this is my body.”
NLT.23: And he took a cup of wine and gave thanks to God for it. He gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
NLT.24: And he said to them, “This is my blood, which confirms the covenant between God and his people. It is poured out as a sacrifice for many.
NLT.25: I tell you the truth, I will not drink wine again until the day I drink it new in the Kingdom of God.”
NLT.26: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives.
NLT.27:
On the way, Jesus told them, “All of you will desert me. For the Scriptures say, ‘God will strike the Shepherd,
and the sheep will be scattered.’
NLT.28: But after I am raised from the dead, I will go ahead of you to Galilee and meet you there.”
NLT.29: Peter said to him, “Even if everyone else deserts you, I never will.”
NLT.30: Jesus replied, “I tell you the truth, Peter — this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny three times that you even know me.”
NLT.31: “No!” Peter declared emphatically. “Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you!” And all the others vowed the same.
NLT.32: They went to the olive grove called Gethsemane, and Jesus said, “Sit here while I go and pray.”
NLT.33: He took Peter, James, and John with him, and he became deeply troubled and distressed.
NLT.34: He told them, “My soul is crushed with grief to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with me.”
NLT.35: He went on a little farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if it were possible, the awful hour awaiting him might pass him by.
NLT.36: “Abba, Father,” he cried out, “everything is possible for you. Please take this cup of suffering away from me. Yet I want your will to be done, not mine.”
NLT.37: Then he returned and found the disciples asleep. He said to Peter, “Simon, are you asleep? Couldn’t you watch with me even one hour?
NLT.38: Keep watch and pray, so that you will not give in to temptation. For the spirit is willing, but the body is weak.”
NLT.39: Then Jesus left them again and prayed the same prayer as before.
NLT.40: When he returned to them again, he found them sleeping, for they couldn’t keep their eyes open. And they didn’t know what to say.
NLT.41: When he returned to them the third time, he said, “Go ahead and sleep. Have your rest. But no — the time has come. The Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
NLT.42: Up, let’s be going. Look, my betrayer is here!”
NLT.43: And immediately, even as Jesus said this, Judas, one of the twelve disciples, arrived with a crowd of men armed with swords and clubs. They had been sent by the leading priests, the teachers of religious law, and the elders.
NLT.44: The traitor, Judas, had given them a prearranged signal: “You will know which one to arrest when I greet him with a kiss. Then you can take him away under guard.”
NLT.45: As soon as they arrived, Judas walked up to Jesus. “Rabbi!” he exclaimed, and gave him the kiss.
NLT.46: Then the others grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
NLT.47: But one of the men with Jesus pulled out his sword and struck the high priest’s slave, slashing off his ear.
NLT.48: Jesus asked them, “Am I some dangerous revolutionary, that you come with swords and clubs to arrest me?
NLT.49: Why didn’t you arrest me in the Temple? I was there among you teaching every day. But these things are happening to fulfill what the Scriptures say about me.”
NLT.50: Then all his disciples deserted him and ran away.
NLT.51: One young man following behind was clothed only in a long linen shirt. When the mob tried to grab him,
NLT.52: he slipped out of his shirt and ran away naked.
NLT.53: They took Jesus to the high priest’s home where the leading priests, the elders, and the teachers of religious law had gathered.
NLT.54: Meanwhile, Peter followed him at a distance and went right into the high priest’s courtyard. There he sat with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
NLT.55: Inside, the leading priests and the entire high council were trying to find evidence against Jesus, so they could put him to death. But they couldn’t find any.
NLT.56: Many false witnesses spoke against him, but they contradicted each other.
NLT.57: Finally, some men stood up and gave this false testimony:
NLT.58: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this Temple made with human hands, and in three days I will build another, made without human hands.’”
NLT.59: But even then they didn’t get their stories straight!
NLT.60: Then the high priest stood up before the others and asked Jesus, “Well, aren’t you going to answer these charges? What do you have to say for yourself?”
NLT.61: But Jesus was silent and made no reply. Then the high priest asked him, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?”
NLT.62: Jesus said, “I AM. And you will see the Son of Man seated in the place of power at God’s right hand and coming on the clouds of heaven. ”
NLT.63: Then the high priest tore his clothing to show his horror and said, “Why do we need other witnesses?
NLT.64: You have all heard his blasphemy. What is your verdict?” “Guilty!” they all cried. “He deserves to die!”
NLT.65: Then some of them began to spit at him, and they blindfolded him and beat him with their fists. “Prophesy to us,” they jeered. And the guards slapped him as they took him away.
NLT.66: Meanwhile, Peter was in the courtyard below. One of the servant girls who worked for the high priest came by
NLT.67: and noticed Peter warming himself at the fire. She looked at him closely and said, “You were one of those with Jesus of Nazareth. ”
NLT.68: But Peter denied it. “I don’t know what you’re talking about,” he said, and he went out into the entryway. Just then, a rooster crowed.
NLT.69: When the servant girl saw him standing there, she began telling the others, “This man is definitely one of them!”
NLT.70: But Peter denied it again. A little later some of the other bystanders confronted Peter and said, “You must be one of them, because you are a Galilean.”
NLT.71: Peter swore, “A curse on me if I’m lying — I don’t know this man you’re talking about!”
NLT.72: And immediately the rooster crowed the second time. Suddenly, Jesus’ words flashed through Peter’s mind: “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny three times that you even know me.” And he broke down and wept.

WEB.1: It was now two days before the feast of the Passover and the unleavened bread, and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might seize him by deception, and kill him.
WEB.2: For they said, “Not during the feast, because there might be a riot of the people.”
WEB.3: While he was at Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster jar of ointment of pure nard — very costly. She broke the jar, and poured it over his head.
WEB.4: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, saying, “Why has this ointment been wasted?
WEB.5: For this might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii,  and given to the poor.” They grumbled against her.
WEB.6: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for me.
WEB.7: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want to, you can do them good; but you will not always have me.
WEB.8: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body beforehand for the burying.
WEB.9: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News may be preached throughout the whole world, that which this woman has done will also be spoken of for a memorial of her.”
WEB.10: Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests, that he might deliver him to them.
WEB.11: They, when they heard it, were glad, and promised to give him money. He sought how he might conveniently deliver him.
WEB.12: On the first day of unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare that you may eat the Passover?”
WEB.13: He sent two of his disciples, and said to them, “Go into the city, and there you will meet a man carrying a pitcher of water. Follow him,
WEB.14: and wherever he enters in, tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
WEB.15: He will himself show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Get ready for us there.”
WEB.16: His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found things as he had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.
WEB.17: When it was evening he came with the twelve.
WEB.18: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me — he who eats with me.”
WEB.19: They began to be sorrowful, and to ask him one by one, “Surely not I?” And another said, “Surely not I?”
WEB.20: He answered them, “It is one of the twelve, he who dips with me in the dish.
WEB.21: For the Son of Man goes, even as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”
WEB.22: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had blessed, he broke it, and gave to them, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body.”
WEB.23: He took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave to them. They all drank of it.
WEB.24: He said to them, “This is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many.
WEB.25: Most certainly I tell you, I will no more drink of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it anew in God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.26: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
WEB.27: Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
WEB.28: However, after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”
WEB.29: But Peter said to him, “Although all will be offended, yet I will not.”
WEB.30: Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you, that you today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”
WEB.31: But he spoke all the more, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” They all said the same thing.
WEB.32: They came to a place which was named Gethsemane. He said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I pray.”
WEB.33: He took with him Peter, James, and John, and began to be greatly troubled and distressed.
WEB.34: He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here, and watch.”
WEB.35: He went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass away from him.
WEB.36: He said, “Abba,  Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire.”
WEB.37: He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn’t you watch one hour?
WEB.38: Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
WEB.39: Again he went away, and prayed, saying the same words.
WEB.40: Again he returned, and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they didn’t know what to answer him.
WEB.41: He came the third time, and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. It is enough. The hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
WEB.42: Arise, let us be going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”
WEB.43: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came — and with him a multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.
WEB.44: Now he who betrayed him had given them a sign, saying, “Whomever I will kiss, that is he. Seize him, and lead him away safely.”
WEB.45: When he had come, immediately he came to him, and said, “Rabbi! Rabbi!” and kissed him.
WEB.46: They laid their hands on him, and seized him.
WEB.47: But a certain one of those who stood by drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
WEB.48: Jesus answered them, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to seize me?
WEB.49: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me. But this is so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.”
WEB.50: They all left him, and fled.
WEB.51: A certain young man followed him, having a linen cloth thrown around himself, over his naked body. The young men grabbed him,
WEB.52: but he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.
WEB.53: They led Jesus away to the high priest. All the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes came together with him.
WEB.54: Peter had followed him from a distance, until he came into the court of the high priest. He was sitting with the officers, and warming himself in the light of the fire.
WEB.55: Now the chief priests and the whole council sought witnesses against Jesus to put him to death, and found none.
WEB.56: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony didn’t agree with each other.
WEB.57: Some stood up, and gave false testimony against him, saying,
WEB.58: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’”
WEB.59: Even so, their testimony did not agree.
WEB.60: The high priest stood up in the middle, and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it which these testify against you?”
WEB.61: But he stayed quiet, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”
WEB.62: Jesus said, “I am. You will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of the sky.”
WEB.63: The high priest tore his clothes, and said, “What further need have we of witnesses?
WEB.64: You have heard the blasphemy! What do you think?” They all condemned him to be worthy of death.
WEB.65: Some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with fists, and to tell him, “Prophesy!” The officers struck him with the palms of their hands.
WEB.66: As Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the maids of the high priest came,
WEB.67: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him, and said, “You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus!”
WEB.68: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know, nor understand what you are saying.” He went out on the porch, and the rooster crowed.
WEB.69: The maid saw him, and began again to tell those who stood by, “This is one of them.”
WEB.70: But he again denied it. After a little while again those who stood by said to Peter, “You truly are one of them, for you are a Galilean, and your speech shows it.”
WEB.71: But he began to curse, and to swear, “I don’t know this man of whom you speak!”
WEB.72: The rooster crowed the second time. Peter remembered the word, how that Jesus said to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” When he thought about that, he wept.

CSB17.8: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body <sup>ⓛ</sup> in advance for burial. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.9: Truly I tell you, <sup>ⓝ</sup> wherever the gospel <sup>ⓐ</sup> is proclaimed in the whole world, <sup>ⓑ</sup> what she has done will also be told in memory of her.”
CSB17.10: Then <sup>ⓒ</sup> Judas Iscariot, <sup>ⓓ</sup> one of the Twelve, <sup>ⓔ</sup> went to the chief priests <sup>ⓕ</sup> to betray Jesus <sup>ⓖ</sup> to them.
CSB17.11: And when they heard this, they were glad and promised to give him money. <sup>ⓗ</sup> So he started looking for a good opportunity to betray <sup>ⓘ</sup> him.
CSB17.12: On <sup>ⓙ</sup> the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrifice the Passover lamb, <sup>ⓚ</sup> his disciples <sup>ⓛ</sup> asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare the Passover so that you may eat it?”
CSB17.13: So he sent two of his disciples and told them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him.
CSB17.14: Wherever he enters, tell the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher <sup>ⓜ</sup> says, “Where is my guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
CSB17.15: He will show you a large room upstairs, furnished and ready. Make the preparations for us there.”
CSB17.16: So the disciples <sup>ⓝ</sup> went out, entered the city, and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
CSB17.17: When <sup>ⓐ</sup> evening came, he arrived with the Twelve. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.18: While they were reclining and eating, Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me — one who is eating with me.”
CSB17.19: They began to be distressed and to say to him one by one, “Surely not I?”
CSB17.20: He said to them, “It is one of the Twelve — the one who is dipping bread in the bowl with me.
CSB17.21: For the Son of Man <sup>ⓒ</sup> will go just as it is written <sup>ⓓ</sup> about him, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but woe <sup>ⓕ</sup> to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for him if he had not been born.”
CSB17.22: As <sup>ⓖ</sup> they were eating, he took bread, <sup>ⓗ</sup> blessed and broke <sup>ⓘ</sup> it, gave it to them, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and said, “Take it; this is my body.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.23: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks, <sup>ⓛ</sup> he gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
CSB17.24: He said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>which is poured out <sup>ⓝ</sup>for many. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.25: Truly I tell you, <sup>ⓑ</sup>I will no longer drink of the fruit of the vine <sup>ⓒ</sup>until that day when I drink it new <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>in the kingdom of God.” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.26: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.27:
Then ⓖ Jesus said to them, “All of you will fall away, [5]because it is written: ⓗ I will strike the shepherd,
and the sheep ⓘ will be scattered. [6] ⓙ
CSB17.28: But after I have risen, <sup>ⓚ</sup> I will go ahead of you to Galilee.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.29: Peter <sup>ⓜ</sup> told him, “Even if everyone falls away, I will not.”
CSB17.30: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus said to him, “today, this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.31: But he kept insisting, “If I have to die with you, I will never deny you.” And they all said the same thing.
CSB17.32: Then <sup>ⓐ</sup> they came to a place named Gethsemane, and he told his disciples, <sup>ⓑ</sup> “Sit here while I pray.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.33: He took Peter, <sup>ⓓ</sup> James, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and John <sup>ⓕ</sup> with him, and he began to be deeply distressed and troubled.
CSB17.34: He said to them, “I am deeply grieved <sup>[7]</sup>to the point of death. Remain here and stay awake.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.35: He went a little farther, fell to the ground, and prayed that if it were possible, the hour <sup>ⓗ</sup> might pass from him.
CSB17.36: And he said, “Abba, [8]Father! ⓘAll things are possible ⓙfor you. Take this cup ⓚaway from me. Nevertheless, not what I will, but what you will.”
CSB17.37: Then he came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, “Simon, <sup>ⓛ</sup> are you sleeping? <sup>ⓜ</sup> Couldn’t you stay awake one hour?
CSB17.38: Stay awake and pray <sup>ⓝ</sup>so that you won’t enter into temptation. <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup>The spirit <sup>ⓑ</sup>is willing, but the flesh <sup>ⓒ</sup>is weak.”
CSB17.39: Once again he went away and prayed, saying the same thing.
CSB17.40: And again he came and found them sleeping, because they could not keep their eyes open. They did not know what to say to him. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.41: Then he came a third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough! The time has come. See, the Son of Man <sup>ⓔ</sup>is betrayed into the hands of sinners. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.42: Get up;  <sup>ⓖ</sup>let’s go. See, my betrayer is near.”
CSB17.43: While <sup>ⓗ</sup> he was still speaking, Judas, <sup>ⓘ</sup> one of the Twelve, <sup>ⓙ</sup> suddenly arrived. With him was a mob, with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and the elders. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.44: His betrayer had given them a signal. “The one I kiss,” he said, “he’s the one; arrest him and take him away under guard.”
CSB17.45: So when he came, immediately he went up to Jesus and said, “Rabbi!” <sup>ⓜ</sup> and kissed him.
CSB17.46: They took hold of him and arrested him.
CSB17.47: One of those who stood by drew his sword, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his ear.
CSB17.48: Jesus said to them, “Have you come out with swords and clubs, as if I were a criminal, <sup>[10]</sup>to capture me?
CSB17.49: Every day I was among you, teaching in the temple, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and you didn’t arrest me. But the Scriptures <sup>ⓐ</sup> must be fulfilled.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.50: Then they all deserted him and ran away. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.51: Now a certain young man, wearing nothing but a linen cloth, was following him. They caught hold of him,
CSB17.52: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.53: They <sup>ⓔ</sup> led Jesus away to the high priest, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes <sup>ⓖ</sup> assembled. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.54: Peter <sup>ⓘ</sup> followed him at a distance, right into the high priest’s courtyard. He was sitting with the servants, <sup>[11]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> warming himself by the fire.
CSB17.55: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin <sup>ⓚ</sup> were looking for testimony against Jesus to put him to death, <sup>ⓛ</sup> but they could not find any.
CSB17.56: For many were giving false testimony <sup>ⓜ</sup> against him, and the testimonies did not agree.
CSB17.57: Some stood up and gave false testimony against him, stating,
CSB17.58: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple made with human hands, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and in three days I will build another not made by hands.’”
CSB17.59: Yet their testimony did not agree <sup>ⓐ</sup> even on this.
CSB17.60: Then the high priest stood up before them all and questioned Jesus, “Don’t you have an answer to what these men are testifying against you?”
CSB17.61: But he kept silent and did not answer. <sup>ⓑ</sup> Again the high priest questioned him, “Are you the Messiah, <sup>ⓒ</sup> the Son <sup>ⓓ</sup> of the Blessed <sup>ⓔ</sup> One?”
CSB17.62: “I am,” ⓕ said Jesus, “and you will see the Son of Man ⓖ seated at the right hand ⓗ of Power and coming with the clouds ⓘ of heaven.” [12] ⓙ
CSB17.63: Then the high priest <sup>ⓚ</sup> tore his robes <sup>ⓛ</sup> and said, “Why do we still need witnesses? <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.64: You have heard the blasphemy. <sup>ⓝ</sup> What is your decision?” They all condemned him as deserving death. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.65: Then some began to spit on him, to blindfold him, and to beat him, saying, “Prophesy!” <sup>ⓑ</sup> The temple servants <sup>ⓒ</sup> also took him and slapped him.
CSB17.66: While <sup>ⓓ</sup> Peter <sup>ⓔ</sup> was in the courtyard below, one of the high priest’s maidservants came.
CSB17.67: When she saw Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, “You also were with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.68: But he denied it: “I don’t know or understand what you’re talking about.” Then he went out to the entryway, <sup>[13]</sup> and a rooster crowed. <sup>[14]</sup>
CSB17.69: When the maidservant saw him again, she began to tell those standing nearby, “This man is one of them.”
CSB17.70: But again he denied it. After a little while those standing there said to Peter again, “You certainly are one of them, since you’re also a Galilean.” <sup>[15]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.71: Then he started to curse and swear, <sup>ⓗ</sup> “I don’t know this man you’re talking about!”
CSB17.72: Immediately a rooster crowed a second time, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and Peter remembered when Jesus had spoken the word to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” And he broke down and wept.

AMP.1: It was now two days before the Passover and [the festival of] Unleavened Bread, and the chief priests and the scribes were searching for a deceitful way to arrest Jesus and kill Him;
AMP.2: but they were saying, "Not during the festival, for the people might riot."
AMP.3: While He was in Bethany [as a guest] at the home of Simon the leper, and reclining at the table, a woman came with an alabaster vial of very costly and precious perfume of pure nard; and she broke the vial and poured the perfume over His head.
AMP.4: But there were some who were indignantly remarking to one another, "Why has this perfume been wasted?
AMP.5: For this perfume might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii [a laborer's wages for almost a year], and the money given to the poor." And they scolded her.
AMP.6: But Jesus said, "Let her alone; why are you bothering her and causing trouble? She has done a good and beautiful thing to Me.
AMP.7: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you wish you can do something good to them; but you will not always have Me.
AMP.8: She has done what she could; she has anointed My body beforehand for the burial.
AMP.9: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, wherever the good news [regarding salvation] is proclaimed throughout the world, what she has done will be told in memory of her."
AMP.10: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve [disciples], went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.
AMP.11: When they heard this they were delighted, and promised to give him money. And he began looking for an opportune time to betray Jesus.
AMP.12: On the first day [of the festival] of Unleavened Bread, when [as was customary] they sacrificed the Passover lamb, His disciples asked Him, "Where do You want us to go and prepare for You to eat the Passover?"
AMP.13: And He sent two of His disciples, saying to them, "Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him;
AMP.14: and say to the owner of the house he enters, 'The Teacher asks, "Where is My guest room in which I may eat the Passover with My disciples?"'
AMP.15: He will show you a large upstairs room, furnished and ready [with carpets and dining couches]; prepare [the supper] for us there."
AMP.16: The disciples left and went to the city and found everything just as He had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
AMP.17: When it was evening, He came with the twelve [disciples].
AMP.18: While they were reclining at the table, Jesus said, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you that one of you will betray Me—one who is eating with Me."
AMP.19: They began to be grieved and deeply distressed and to say to Him one by one, "Surely not I?"
AMP.20: And He replied, "It is one of the twelve [disciples], one who is dipping bread in the bowl with Me.
AMP.21: For the Son of Man goes [to the cross] just as it is written [in Scripture] of Him; but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been good for that man if he had not been born."
AMP.22: While they were eating, Jesus took bread and blessed it [giving thanks and praise], and He broke it, and gave it to them and said, "Take it. This is My body."
AMP.23: And when He had taken a cup [of wine] and given thanks, He gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
AMP.24: And He said to them, "This is My blood of the [new] covenant, [My blood] which is being poured out for many [for the forgiveness of sins].
AMP.25: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God."
AMP.26: After they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
AMP.27: Jesus said to them, "You will all fall away [and be ashamed and be afraid to be associated with Me as disciples], because it is written, 'I will strike the Shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'
AMP.28: But after I have been raised [from the dead], I will go ahead of you to Galilee."
AMP.29: But Peter said to Him, "Even if they all fall away [and desert You, ashamed and afraid of being associated with You], yet I will not [do so]!"
AMP.30: Jesus said to him, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, this very night, before a rooster crows twice, you will deny [that you even know] Me three times."
AMP.31: But Peter kept saying insistently, "If I have to die with You, I will not deny You!" And they all were saying the same thing as well.
AMP.32: Then they went to a place called Gethsemane; and Jesus said to His disciples, "Sit down here until I have prayed."
AMP.33: He took Peter and James and John with Him, and He began to be deeply distressed and troubled [extremely anguished at the prospect of what was to come].
AMP.34: And He said to them, "My soul is deeply grieved and overwhelmed with sorrow, to the point of death; remain here and keep watch."
AMP.35: After going a little farther, He fell to the ground [distressed by the weight of His spiritual burden] and began to pray that if it were possible [in the Father's will], the hour [of suffering and death for the sins of mankind] might pass from Him.
AMP.36: He was saying, " Abba, Father! All things are possible for You; take this cup [of judgment] away from Me; but not what I will, but what You will."
AMP.37: And He came back and found them sleeping, and He said to Peter, "Simon, are you asleep? Were you unable to keep watch for one hour?
AMP.38: Keep [actively] watching and praying so that you do not come into temptation; the spirit is willing, but the body is weak."
AMP.39: He went away again and prayed, saying the same words.
AMP.40: And again He came back and found them sleeping, because their eyes were very heavy; and they did not know how to answer Him.
AMP.41: He came back a third time and said to them, "Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough [of that]! The hour has come. Look, the Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of sinners.
AMP.42: Get up, let us go. Look, my betrayer is near!"
AMP.43: And at once, while He was still speaking, Judas [Iscariot], one of the twelve [disciples], came up, and with him a crowd of men with swords and clubs, who came from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders [of the Sanhedrin].
AMP.44: Now the betrayer had given them a signal, saying, "Whomever I kiss, He is the one; seize Him and lead Him away safely [under guard]."
AMP.45: When Judas came, immediately he went up to Jesus and said, "Rabbi (Master)!" and he kissed Him [forcefully].
AMP.46: They laid hands on Him and seized Him.
AMP.47: But one of the bystanders [Simon Peter] drew his sword and struck [Malchus] the slave of the high priest and cut off his ear.
AMP.48: Jesus said to them, "Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest Me, as you would against a robber?
AMP.49: Day after day I was with you, teaching in the [courts and porches of the] temple, and you did not seize Me; but this has happened so that the Scriptures would be fulfilled."
AMP.50: Then all of His disciples abandoned Him and fled.
AMP.51: A young man was following Him, wearing [only] a linen sheet over his naked body; and some men seized him.
AMP.52: But pulling free of the linen sheet, he escaped [from them] naked.
AMP.53: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court) gathered together.
AMP.54: Peter had followed Him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest; and he was sitting with the officers [guards and servants] and warming himself at the fire.
AMP.55: Now the chief priests and the entire Council (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court) were trying to obtain testimony against Jesus [which they could use] to have Him [condemned and] executed, but they were not finding any.
AMP.56: For many [people] were giving false testimony against Him, but their testimonies were not consistent.
AMP.57: Some stood up and began to give false testimony against Him, saying,
AMP.58: "We heard Him say, 'I will destroy this temple (sanctuary) that was made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.'"
AMP.59: Not even in this respect was their testimony consistent.
AMP.60: The high priest stood up and came forward and asked Jesus, "Have You no answer to give [in response] to what these men are testifying against You?"
AMP.61: But Jesus kept silent and gave no answer at all. Again the high priest was questioning Him, and saying to Him, "Are You the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), the Son of the Blessed One?"
AMP.62: Jesus said, "I am; and you will [all] see the Son of Man seated [with authority] at the right hand of Power (the Father), and coming with the clouds of heaven."
AMP.63: Then tearing his robe [to express his indignation], the high priest said, "What further need do we have of witnesses?
AMP.64: You have heard the blasphemy [that is, His claim to be the Son of God]. What is your decision?" And they all condemned Him to be [guilty and] deserving of death.
AMP.65: And some began to spit on Him, and to blindfold Him, and to beat Him with their fists, and to say to Him, "Prophesy [by telling us who hit you]!" Then the officers took custody of Him and struck him in the face.
AMP.66: While Peter was down below in the courtyard, one of the servant-girls of the high priest came,
AMP.67: and when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked intently at him and said, "You were with Jesus the Nazarene, too."
AMP.68: But he denied it, saying, "I neither know nor understand what you are talking about." Then he went out [of the courtyard] to the porch,[and a rooster crowed.]
AMP.69: The servant-girl saw him, and began once more to tell the bystanders, "This [man] is one of them."
AMP.70: But again he denied it. After a little while, the bystanders again said to Peter, "You are in fact one of them, for [it is clear from your accent, that] you are a Galilean, too."
AMP.71: But he began to invoke a curse [on himself] and to swear [an oath], "I do not know this man you are talking about!"
AMP.72: Immediately a rooster crowed the second time. And Peter remembered what Jesus said to him: "Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times." And thinking of this, he began weeping [in anguish].

MSG.1: In only two days the eight-day Festival of Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread would begin. The high priests and religion scholars were looking for a way they could seize Jesus by stealth and kill him.
MSG.2: They agreed that it should not be done during Passover Week. "We don't want the crowds up in arms," they said.
MSG.3: Jesus was at Bethany, a guest of Simon the Leper. While he was eating dinner, a woman came up carrying a bottle of very expensive perfume. Opening the bottle, she poured it on his head.
MSG.4: Some of the guests became furious among themselves. "That's criminal! A sheer waste!
MSG.5: This perfume could have been sold for well over a year's wages and handed out to the poor." They swelled up in anger, nearly bursting with indignation over her.
MSG.6: But Jesus said, "Let her alone. Why are you giving her a hard time? She has just done something wonderfully significant for me.
MSG.7: You will have the poor with you every day for the rest of your lives. Whenever you feel like it, you can do something for them. Not so with me.
MSG.8: She did what she could when she could—she pre-anointed my body for burial.
MSG.9: And you can be sure that wherever in the whole world the Message is preached, what she just did is going to be talked about admiringly."
MSG.10: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the cabal of high priests, determined to betray him.
MSG.11: They couldn't believe their ears, and promised to pay him well. He started looking for just the right moment to hand him over.
MSG.12: On the first of the Days of Unleavened Bread, the day they prepare the Passover sacrifice, his disciples asked him, "Where do you want us to go and make preparations so you can eat the Passover meal?"
MSG.13: He directed two of his disciples, "Go into the city. A man carrying a water jug will meet you. Follow him.
MSG.14: Ask the owner of whichever house he enters, 'The Teacher wants to know, Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples?'
MSG.15: He will show you a spacious second-story room, swept and ready. Prepare for us there."
MSG.16: The disciples left, came to the city, found everything just as he had told them, and prepared the Passover meal.
MSG.17: After sunset he came with the Twelve.
MSG.18: As they were at the supper table eating, Jesus said, "I have something hard but important to say to you: One of you is going to hand me over to the conspirators, one who at this moment is eating with me."
MSG.19: Stunned, they started asking, one after another, "It isn't me, is it?"
MSG.20: He said, "It's one of the Twelve, one who eats with me out of the same bowl.
MSG.21: In one sense, it turns out that the Son of Man is entering into a way of treachery well-marked by the Scriptures—no surprises here. In another sense, the man who turns him in, turns traitor to the Son of Man—better never to have been born than do this!"
MSG.22: In the course of their meal, having taken and blessed the bread, he broke it and gave it to them. Then he said, Take, this is my body.
MSG.23: Taking the chalice, he gave it to them, thanking God, and they all drank from it.
MSG.24: He said, This is my blood, God's new covenant, Poured out for many people.
MSG.25: "I'll not be drinking wine again until the new day when I drink it in the kingdom of God."
MSG.26: They sang a hymn and then went directly to Mount Olives.
MSG.27: Jesus told them, "You're all going to feel that your world is falling apart and that it's my fault. There's a Scripture that says, I will strike the shepherd; The sheep will go helter-skelter.
MSG.28: "But after I am raised up, I will go ahead of you, leading the way to Galilee."
MSG.29: Peter blurted out, "Even if everyone else is ashamed of you when things fall to pieces, I won't be."
MSG.30: Jesus said, "Don't be so sure. Today, this very night in fact, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times."
MSG.31: He blustered in protest, "Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you." All the others said the same thing.
MSG.32: They came to an area called Gethsemane. Jesus told his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."
MSG.33: He took Peter, James, and John with him. He plunged into a sinkhole of dreadful agony.
MSG.34: He told them, "I feel bad enough right now to die. Stay here and keep vigil with me."
MSG.35: Going a little ahead, he fell to the ground and prayed for a way out:
MSG.36: "Papa, Father, you can—can't you?—get me out of this. Take this cup away from me. But please, not what I want—what do you want?"
MSG.37: He came back and found them sound asleep. He said to Peter, "Simon, you went to sleep on me? Can't you stick it out with me a single hour?
MSG.38: Stay alert, be in prayer, so you don't enter the danger zone without even knowing it. Don't be naive. Part of you is eager, ready for anything in God; but another part is as lazy as an old dog sleeping by the fire."
MSG.39: He then went back and prayed the same prayer.
MSG.40: Returning, he again found them sound asleep. They simply couldn't keep their eyes open, and they didn't have a plausible excuse.
MSG.41: He came back a third time and said, "Are you going to sleep all night? No—you've slept long enough. Time's up. The Son of Man is about to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
MSG.42: Get up. Let's get going. My betrayer has arrived."
MSG.43: No sooner were the words out of his mouth when Judas, the one out of the Twelve, showed up, and with him a gang of ruffians, sent by the high priests, religion scholars, and leaders, brandishing swords and clubs.
MSG.44: The betrayer had worked out a signal with them: "The one I kiss, that's the one—seize him. Make sure he doesn't get away."
MSG.45: He went straight to Jesus and said, "Rabbi!" and kissed him.
MSG.46: The others then grabbed him and roughed him up.
MSG.47: One of the men standing there unsheathed his sword, swung, and came down on the Chief Priest's servant, lopping off the man's ear.
MSG.48: Jesus said to them, "What is this, coming after me with swords and clubs as if I were a dangerous criminal?
MSG.49: Day after day I've been sitting in the Temple teaching, and you never so much as lifted a hand against me. What you in fact have done is confirm the prophetic writings."
MSG.50: All the disciples cut and ran.
MSG.51: A young man was following along. All he had on was a bedsheet. Some of the men grabbed him
MSG.52: but he got away, running off naked, leaving them holding the sheet.
MSG.53: They led Jesus to the Chief Priest, where the high priests, religious leaders, and scholars had gathered together.
MSG.54: Peter followed at a safe distance until they got to the Chief Priest's courtyard, where he mingled with the servants and warmed himself at the fire.
MSG.55: The high priests conspiring with the Jewish Council looked high and low for evidence against Jesus by which they could sentence him to death. They found nothing.
MSG.56: Plenty of people were willing to bring in false charges, but nothing added up, and they ended up canceling each other out.
MSG.57: Then a few of them stood up and lied:
MSG.58: "We heard him say, 'I am going to tear down this Temple, built by hard labor, and in three days build another without lifting a hand.'"
MSG.59: But even they couldn't agree exactly.
MSG.60: In the middle of this, the Chief Priest stood up and asked Jesus, "What do you have to say to the accusation?"
MSG.61: Jesus was silent. He said nothing. The Chief Priest tried again, this time asking, "Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed?"
MSG.62: Jesus said, "Yes, I am, and you'll see it yourself: The Son of Man seated At the right hand of the Mighty One, Arriving on the clouds of heaven."
MSG.63: The Chief Priest lost his temper. Ripping his clothes, he yelled, "Did you hear that? After that do we need witnesses?
MSG.64: You heard the blasphemy. Are you going to stand for it?" They condemned him, one and all. The sentence: death.

NIV.1:
Jesus Anointed at Bethany
Now the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread were only two days away, and the chief priests and the teachers of the law were looking for some sly way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
NIV.2: “But not during the Feast,” they said, “or the people may riot.”
NIV.3: While he was in Bethany, reclining at the table in the home of a man known as Simon the Leper, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very expensive perfume, made of pure nard. She broke the jar and poured the perfume on his head.
NIV.4: Some of those present were saying indignantly to one another, “Why this waste of perfume?
NIV.5: It could have been sold for more than a year’s wages and the money given to the poor.” And they rebuked her harshly.
NIV.6: “Leave her alone,” said Jesus. “Why are you bothering her? She has done a beautiful thing to me.
NIV.7: The poor you will always have with you, and you can help them any time you want. But you will not always have me.
NIV.8: She did what she could. She poured perfume on my body beforehand to prepare for my burial.
NIV.9: I tell you the truth, wherever the gospel is preached throughout the world, what she has done will also be told, in memory of her.”
NIV.10: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.
NIV.11: They were delighted to hear this and promised to give him money. So he watched for an opportunity to hand him over.
NIV.12:
The Lord’s Supper
On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, when it was customary to sacrifice the Passover lamb, Jesus’ disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and make preparations for you to eat the Passover?”
NIV.13: So he sent two of his disciples, telling them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him.
NIV.14: Say to the owner of the house he enters, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?’
NIV.15: He will show you a large upper room, furnished and ready. Make preparations for us there.”
NIV.16: The disciples left, went into the city and found things just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover.
NIV.17: When evening came, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
NIV.18: While they were reclining at the table eating, he said, “I tell you the truth, one of you will betray me—one who is eating with me.”
NIV.19: They were saddened, and one by one they said to him, “Surely not I?”
NIV.20: “It is one of the Twelve,” he replied, “one who dips bread into the bowl with me.
NIV.21: The Son of Man will go just as it is written about him. But woe to that man who betrays the Son of Man! It would be better for him if he had not been born.”
NIV.22: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to his disciples, saying, “Take it; this is my body.”
NIV.23: Then he took the cup, gave thanks and offered it to them, and they all drank from it.
NIV.24: “This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many,” he said to them.
NIV.25: “I tell you the truth, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it anew in the kingdom of God.”
NIV.26: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
NIV.27:
Jesus Predicts Peter’s Denial
“You will all fall away,” Jesus told them, “for it is written:
“‘I will strike the shepherd,
and the sheep will be scattered.’
NIV.28: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee.”
NIV.29: Peter declared, “Even if all fall away, I will not.”
NIV.30: “I tell you the truth,” Jesus answered, “today—yes, tonight—before the rooster crows twice you yourself will disown me three times.”
NIV.31: But Peter insisted emphatically, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never disown you.” And all the others said the same.
NIV.32:
Gethsemane
They went to a place called Gethsemane, and Jesus said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
NIV.33: He took Peter, James and John along with him, and he began to be deeply distressed and troubled.
NIV.34: “My soul is overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death,” he said to them. “Stay here and keep watch.”
NIV.35: Going a little farther, he fell to the ground and prayed that if possible the hour might pass from him.
NIV.36: “Abba, Father,” he said, “everything is possible for you. Take this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will.”
NIV.37: Then he returned to his disciples and found them sleeping. “Simon,” he said to Peter, “are you asleep? Could you not keep watch for one hour?
NIV.38: Watch and pray so that you will not fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the body is weak.”
NIV.39: Once more he went away and prayed the same thing.
NIV.40: When he came back, he again found them sleeping, because their eyes were heavy. They did not know what to say to him.
NIV.41: Returning the third time, he said to them, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough! The hour has come. Look, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
NIV.42: Rise! Let us go! Here comes my betrayer!”
NIV.43:
Jesus Arrested
Just as he was speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, appeared. With him was a crowd armed with swords and clubs, sent from the chief priests, the teachers of the law, and the elders.
NIV.44: Now the betrayer had arranged a signal with them: “The one I kiss is the man; arrest him and lead him away under guard.”
NIV.45: Going at once to Jesus, Judas said, “Rabbi!” and kissed him.
NIV.46: The men seized Jesus and arrested him.
NIV.47: Then one of those standing near drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
NIV.48: “Am I leading a rebellion,” said Jesus, “that you have come out with swords and clubs to capture me?
NIV.49: Every day I was with you, teaching in the temple courts, and you did not arrest me. But the Scriptures must be fulfilled.”
NIV.50: Then everyone deserted him and fled.
NIV.51: A young man, wearing nothing but a linen garment, was following Jesus. When they seized him,
NIV.52: he fled naked, leaving his garment behind.
NIV.53:
Before the Sanhedrin
They took Jesus to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders and teachers of the law came together.
NIV.54: Peter followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. There he sat with the guards and warmed himself at the fire.
NIV.55: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for evidence against Jesus so that they could put him to death, but they did not find any.
NIV.56: Many testified falsely against him, but their statements did not agree.
NIV.57: Then some stood up and gave this false testimony against him:
NIV.58: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this man-made temple and in three days will build another, not made by man.’”
NIV.59: Yet even then their testimony did not agree.
NIV.60: Then the high priest stood up before them and asked Jesus, “Are you not going to answer? What is this testimony that these men are bringing against you?”
NIV.61:
But Jesus remained silent and gave no answer.
Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?”
NIV.62: “I am,” said Jesus. “And you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of the Mighty One and coming on the clouds of heaven.”
NIV.63: The high priest tore his clothes. “Why do we need any more witnesses?” he asked.
NIV.64:
“You have heard the blasphemy. What do you think?”
They all condemned him as worthy of death.
NIV.65: Then some began to spit at him; they blindfolded him, struck him with their fists, and said, “Prophesy!” And the guards took him and beat him.
NIV.66:
Peter Disowns Jesus
While Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant girls of the high priest came by.
NIV.67:
When she saw Peter warming himself, she looked closely at him.
“You also were with that Nazarene, Jesus,” she said.
NIV.68: But he denied it. “I don’t know or understand what you’re talking about,” he said, and went out into the entryway.
NIV.69: When the servant girl saw him there, she said again to those standing around, “This fellow is one of them.”
NIV.70:
Again he denied it.
After a little while, those standing near said to Peter, “Surely you are one of them, for you are a Galilean.”
NIV.71: He began to call down curses on himself, and he swore to them, “I don’t know this man you’re talking about.”
NIV.72: Immediately the rooster crowed the second time. Then Peter remembered the word Jesus had spoken to him: “Before the rooster crows twice you will disown me three times.” And he broke down and wept.
